title: carlina vulgaris var. maritima
intro: Carlina vulgaris var. maritima, also known as the sea carline, is a rare and lesser-known variety of the Carlina vulgaris plant, a flowering plant species native to the Mediterranean region. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to treat a variety of health issues, including wounds, digestive problems, and respiratory infections. The plant is believed to have numerous health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting off infections, and promoting digestive health, making it a valuable addition to herbal remedies. Carlina vulgaris var. maritima can be prepared in various forms, including infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, allowing individuals to harness its medicinal properties and reap its health benefits.
